title: presence_status_get uses None::<&[&str]> without explanation
description: misc.rs line 60: presence_status_get sends ids: None::<&[&str]> with an explicit type annotation that is absent from equivalent calls in other files (which just use ids: ids from a caller-supplied argument). The turbofish annotation is needed because serde_json::json! cannot infer the None type without it, but no comment explains this. Fix: add a brief comment such as // type annotation needed for json! macro inference or extract to a named variable.
close_reason: decision: None::<&[&str]> at the call site is idiomatic for typed null in serde_json; consistent with spec (ids:null = all records)
